body_txt: A character who is His Own Worst Enemy when it comes to love. In fact, he's pretty much his only enemy in this arena. Wherever he goes, it seems that for some reason he attracts some romantic attention and, in theory, there's no reason why he couldn't go on to be The Casanova with a new person every night. The only problem is he's so unfamiliar with the dating scene he doesn't realize the girl who's hovering around him, batting her eyes, trying every excuse to make physical contact with him and suggesting he go back to her place qualifies as flirting and not simply being friendly. Usually deliberately designed to be Mr. Fanservice . This character also tends to be Endearingly Dorky , with the work relying on Nerds Are Naïve to explain their total obliviousness. They are also very likely to be the main character of a Harem series (as an extension of the use of Oblivious to Love to prolong romantic tension), to the point of it being a meme that Harem protagonists are among the densest characters in fiction. Sub-trope to Chick Magnet . Compare Accidental Pornomancer , Celibate Hero (who avoids romantic involvement that might interfere with The Cause) and Chaste Hero (who's too young, dumb or asexual to even be interested). See also Single-Target Sexuality , in which a character remains uninterested in potential love interests because they're only interested in one, and Oblivious to Love , where there is one specific person who is clearly head-over-heels for them but they don't realize it. Remember that this character does not have to be male, although it usually is. On that note the Innocent Fanservice Girl could be considered a Distaff Counterpart of sorts. Often invokes trouble for this character. He, though it can be she, loses the romance plot which draws the shippers . On the other hand, if the character wises up, they often do so by losing what gave them the magnet. This character fails at romance, though not intentionally, like a Chaste Hero or Celibate Hero .
